abstract.t: The modern web lacks a reliable mechanism for verifying the identity of HTTP user agents. Existing methods such as User-Agent strings and IP-based attribution are insufficient due to spoofing, shared infrastructure (NAT), and the rise of automated agents. This document specifies SAIP (Signed Agent Identity Protocol), a lightweight, opt-in mechanism for verifiable client identity at the application layer. SAIP enables servers to distinguish between legitimate automated traffic and malicious actors.
